title: Merge Bases, Topology, and Integration Strategies · Repository Repair Bay
summary: A responder inherits a plausible but defective merge topology and strategy decision record; preserve reachable history, isolate the first causal Git-state mismatch, and retain a regression probe. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in merge topology and strategy decision record and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.
Choose fast-forward, no-ff, ff-only, or refusal from release policy and graph evidence rather than branch-name folklore.
Evaluate ort strategy behavior and bounded options for renames, whitespace, subtree shifts, and favoring conflict hunks without replacing semantic review.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Inspect the failure record
steps.instruction:
debug case — Case G67 is a debug in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy.

Inspect the Gita Merge Base Multiple trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compute one or multiple best common ancestors, octopus bases, fork points, and independent tips for complex graphs.

content.text: For Gita Merge Base Multiple, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Every pair of commits has exactly one merge base.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-merge-result-defense-9n5mze" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Merge Result Defense into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-merge-result-defense-9n5mze
# scenario-key: trace-9n5mze probe-ezm5n9 boundary-5mze9n owner-9n5mzex fault-9n5mzez
# before-ref: main=a11ce9n candidate=b00b1ze
# hypothesis: criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor
git merge-base --all release topic
# expected: all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed
# observation: two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration
# changed-case: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base
# after-ref: main=a11ce9n candidate=c0ffeee
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy
# transfer-limit: validate resulting behavior in an authorized disposable clone because graph evidence is not semantic proof
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write the debug evidence note for Gita Merge Result Defense: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Case G75 is a debug in a disposable repository. criss-cross integration yields more than one best common ancestor. The learner must predict all best bases are listed so a synthetic merge base and strategy risk can be reviewed, run git merge-base --all release topic, explain two incomparable bases make a single-base mental model unsafe for this integration, test this changed condition: linearize one side branch and observe the candidate set collapse to one merge base, and preserve this recovery boundary: preserve both tips under refs/integration/before-merge before testing a strategy.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify merge parents, combined diff, resulting tree, tests, release invariants, signatures, and rollback path before publication.
